models.description: Set-covering / set-partitioning master problem of a column-generation crew-pairing scheduler for an airline. Each candidate column is a crew roster (an ordered sequence of duties: execution flights, positioning flights/buses, ground duties) generated by the pricing subproblem. The master selects at most one roster per crew so that every flight and every mandatory ground duty is covered, otherwise an explicit uncovered-slack variable is penalized. Solved as an LP relaxation each column-generation iteration to obtain duals (solve_lp), and finally as a binary integer program (solve_bip). Built incrementally: constraints are created empty at setup and re-formed as columns are added in _add_roster.

sets_indices.name: F
sets_indices.meaning: set of (execution) flights to be covered

sets_indices.name: G
sets_indices.meaning: set of mandatory ground duties (occupancy tasks) to be covered

sets_indices.name: R
sets_indices.meaning: set of generated rosters (columns); each roster r belongs to one crew

sets_indices.name: R_k
sets_indices.source_symbol: [v for r,v in self.roster_vars.items() if r.crew_id == k]

sets_indices.name: R_f
sets_indices.meaning: rosters that cover flight f by executing it (non-positioning)

sets_indices.name: R_g
sets_indices.meaning: rosters that cover ground duty g

parameters.name: c_r
parameters.meaning: cost of roster r = positioning_penalty + overnight_penalty + violation_penalty - flight_reward; flight_reward = FLIGHT_TIME_REWARD * roster_flight_hours / global_duty_days_denominator (global average daily flight-time approximation)

parameters.name: a_{rf}
parameters.meaning: 1 if roster r executes (non-positioning) flight f, else 0; encoded implicitly via membership of v in flight f's covering_vars

parameters.name: b_{rg}
parameters.meaning: 1 if roster r covers ground duty g, else 0; encoded implicitly via membership of v in ground duty g's covering_vars

decision_variables.name: x_r
decision_variables.meaning: 1 if roster r is selected (LP-relaxed to [0,1] in solve_lp; lb=0.1 protective lower bound for initial-solution columns)

decision_variables.name: u_f
decision_variables.meaning: 1 if flight f is left uncovered (slack), in [0,1]

decision_variables.name: z_g
decision_variables.meaning: 1 if ground duty g is left uncovered (slack), in [0,1]

objective.expression_latex: \min \sum_{r \in R} c_r x_r + P_F \sum_{f \in F} u_f + P_G \sum_{g \in G} z_g
objective.expression_plain: minimize total selected-roster cost plus penalties for uncovered flights and uncovered ground duties (objective coefficients set directly via the obj= argument on each addVar; ModelSense = MINIMIZE)

constraints.name: crew_at_most_one_roster
constraints.expression_plain: each crew is assigned at most one roster (created empty at setup, rebuilt as columns are added)

constraints.name: flight_cover
constraints.expression_plain: each flight is executed by exactly one selected roster, or counted as uncovered (set-partitioning equality with slack; initialized as u_f = 1)

constraints.name: ground_duty_cover
constraints.expression_plain: each mandatory ground duty is covered by exactly one selected roster, or counted as uncovered (set-partitioning equality with slack; initialized as z_g = 1)

instances.name: crew.csv
instances.size: 731 rows incl. header (~15 KB); columns crewId,base,stayStation

instances.name: busInfo.csv
instances.size: 4176 rows incl. header (~211 KB); columns id,depaAirport,arriAirport,td,ta (positioning bus legs)

instances.name: crewLegMatch.csv
instances.size: 2351866 rows incl. header (~49 MB); columns crewId,legId (crew-flight qualification matrix)

instances.name: layoverStation.csv
instances.size: 49 rows incl. header (~248 B); column airport

models.notes: This is the column-generation MASTER problem and is the only (MI)LP in the repo (gurobipy appears only in master_problem.py and main.py). The model is built incrementally: at setup the crew constraints are degenerate placeholders (0 <= 0) and the cover constraints start as u_f = 1 / z_g = 1; each call to _add_roster removes and re-adds the affected crew/flight/ground-duty constraints to include the new column. solve_lp relaxes all vars to continuous to read off duals (sigma_k from crew constraints, pi_f from flight constraints, mu_g from ground-duty constraints); solve_bip flips them to binary with TimeLimit=1200s, MIPGap=0.05, MIPFocus=1. Roster cost c_r is data/iteration-dependent: the flight-time reward divides by a global 'duty-days denominator' that is updated each iteration (master_problem.py:209-302), so column objective coefficients are refreshed via _update_all_roster_costs. The branch-and-price branching (set_variable_to_one/zero, master_problem.py:142-207) fixes var bounds rather than adding constraints. Business rules (max duty hours, flight cycles, rest, connection times) are NOT in the master; they are enforced inside the pricing subproblem when generating feasible rosters (see unified_config.py and constraint_checker.py).

extraction_notes: Master problem transcribed directly from gurobipy code in master_problem.py; symbols (x_r, u_f, z_g, c_r, pi/sigma/mu duals) follow the code's own naming and the reduced-cost comments in scoring_system.py:755-764. No published paper found (README is a Chinese project description, no DOI; paper_reference=null). The PRICING SUBPROBLEM is a labeling / resource-constrained shortest-path dynamic program over a time-space network (attention_guided_subproblem_solver.py: heapq-based label expansion with Label states from data_models.py, attention-model-guided beam search), NOT an (MI)LP. Per the hard rules, no LP was invented for it; the column reduced cost it optimizes is c_r - (sum_f pi_f a_{rf} + sum_g mu_g b_{rg} - sigma_k) and a roster is added when reduced_cost < -1e-4 (scoring_system.py:755-778; attention_guided_subproblem_solver.py:1532). Constraint-coefficient parameters a_{rf}, b_{rg} are implicit (membership-encoded in covering_vars lists) rather than explicit matrices, recorded as such. Datasets referenced by path+size from data/ (not inlined); crewLegMatch.csv is ~49 MB. Instance row counts are line counts incl. header.
